script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning. July 23rd, 2026. What a day, huh? I really need this coffee.
script.ja: おはようございます。2026年7月23日。なんというか、すごい日ですね？このコーヒー、本当に必要です。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Good morning, Alex. Yeah, it's pretty gray out there. Drizzle all day, they say.
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。うん、外はかなりどんよりしてるね。一日中霧雨だって。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Drizzle. More like a full-on cloud weeping. My umbrella's barely doing anything.
script.ja: 霧雨。いや、本格的に雲が泣いてる感じですよ。傘もほとんど意味ないし。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: At least it's not a typhoon. The temperature's still high though, 32 degrees.
script.ja: 台風じゃないだけマシかな。でも気温はまだ高いね、32度。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, it feels like that. Muggy. My little one was really restless last night, probably the humidity.
script.ja: ええ、そんな感じです。じめじめして。うちの子、昨夜はすごく寝苦しそうでした。たぶん湿気のせいで。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It makes sense. The air feels heavy.
script.ja: わかる。空気が重いよね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Welcome back to The Daily Tanu-chan Show. Hope everyone's having a slightly less drizzly morning than us.
script.ja: 「The Daily Tanu-chan Show」へようこそ。皆さん、私たちよりは少しマシな霧雨の朝をお過ごしだといいのですが。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Did you see the news about Keisuke Unosawa, the designer? Making ordinary things extraordinary.
script.ja: ニュースで宇野澤啓介さん、あのデザイナーのこと見た？普通のものを非凡にするって話。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, I saw that headline. The guy who didn't go to a fancy design school?
script.ja: あ、その見出しは見た気がします。高級なデザイン学校に行ってないって人ですよね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's the one. His story is quite *exemplary*, actually. He started from nothing.
script.ja: そう、その人。彼の話は、実はとても*模範的*なのよ。何もないところから始めたんだから。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: *Exemplary*? What does that mean, exactly? Like, really good?
script.ja: *模範的*？それって、具体的にどういう意味ですか？すごく良いってこと？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yeah, exactly. Like a perfect example, a model for others. He's a good example for people who think you need a specific background to succeed.
script.ja: ええ、その通り。完璧な手本とか、他の人にとっての模範。特定の経歴がないと成功できないと思っている人たちにとって、彼は良い例だわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, I see. That's true. I feel like my office always wants people from certain universities.
script.ja: ああ、なるほど。確かにそうですね。うちの会社はいつも、特定の大学の人ばかりを欲しがる気がします。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's a common mindset, but not always the best one. And there was something about national treasures, digital reproductions? Hiroko Machida?
script.ja: よくある考え方だけど、いつも最善とは限らないわ。それから、国宝のデジタル複製について何かあったよね？町田裕子さん？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, her academy is launching interior art with digital versions of Japan's treasures. That's kind of cool. Imagine having a digital Hokusai in your living room instead of a poster.
script.ja: ええ、彼女のアカデミーが日本の国宝をデジタル化したインテリアアートを出すとか。それはちょっとかっこいいですね。リビングにポスターじゃなくてデジタルの北斎があるなんて想像すると。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Exactly. It's about accessibility, too. Making those pieces available to more people.
script.ja: その通り。アクセシビリティも重要よね。より多くの人が作品に触れられるようにするのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, and this one! Clothing company Nutte and Axel Mark, doing character costumes from mobile games. Cosplay stuff.
script.ja: あ、あとこれ！アパレル会社のNutteとアクセルマークが、スマホゲームのキャラクター衣装を出すってやつ。コスプレですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: My daughter would be very excited about that. She's always talking about different game characters.
script.ja: うちの娘はすごく喜びそう。いつも色々なゲームのキャラクターの話をしてるから。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, my younger cousin is super into it too. It's huge now, isn't it?
script.ja: ええ、僕のいとこもすごくハマってます。今、すごい人気ですよね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It really is. It's a big part of youth culture. I guess it always has been, in a way.
script.ja: 本当にそうね。若い文化の大きな部分だわ。ある意味、昔からそうだったのかもしれないけど。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Speaking of culture, did you know today is 劇画の日? Gekiga Day.
script.ja: 文化といえば、今日って劇画の日だって知ってた？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Gekiga Day? Oh, I didn't know that. What's gekiga again? It's like manga, right?
script.ja: 劇画の日？ああ、知りませんでした。劇画って何でしたっけ？漫画みたいなものですよね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's a specific style of Japanese comics, more dramatic and realistic than traditional manga. It started in 1964 with Garo magazine.
script.ja: 日本の漫画の特定のスタイルで、伝統的な漫画よりもドラマチックでリアルなの。1964年に「ガロ」という雑誌から始まったのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Garo magazine... I think I've heard of that. Kamui Den came from there, right?
script.ja: ガロ...聞いたことあります。カムイ伝って、そこから生まれたんですよね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's right. Shirato Sanpei's Kamui Den. It pushed the boundaries of what comics could be at the time.
script.ja: そうよ。白土三平の『カムイ伝』。当時の漫画の可能性を広げた作品ね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: So, today's like the *inception* of that movement. The very beginning.
script.ja: じゃあ、今日はそのムーブメントの*始まり*の日なんですね。まさに最初。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You could say that, yes. The start of it.
script.ja: そう言えるわね、ええ。その始まり。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Wow, inception. So like when something first starts?
script.ja: わあ、インセプション。つまり何かが最初に始まる時ってことですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Exactly. The beginning or origin of something.
script.ja: その通り。物事の始まりや起源ね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I learn something new every day here. Even if my brain feels a bit like a wet sponge with this weather.
script.ja: ここでは毎日新しいことを学びますね。この天気で脳が濡れたスポンジみたいになってても。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: My kid just learned to pull himself up on furniture this week. Now he's trying to climb everything. My desk at home isn't safe anymore.
script.ja: うちの子、今週ついに家具によじ登るのを覚えましたよ。もう何でも登ろうとする。家の机ももう安全じゃない。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They move fast at that age. Mine did too.
script.ja: あの時期は成長が早いのよ。うちの子たちもそうだった。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I swear he can teleport sometimes. I turn my back for one second... And then there's the crying. For no reason. Maybe he just likes the sound.
script.ja: たまにテレポートできるんじゃないかと思うんですよ。一瞬目を離した隙に...そして泣く。理由もなく。もしかしたらあの音自体が好きなのかも。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They usually have a reason. You just don't always know what it is.
script.ja: だいたい理由はあるものよ。あなたがいつもそれを知らないだけ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, I guess. I'm just always trying to figure it out. It's a full-time job after the full-time job.
script.ja: ええ、まあそうですね。いつも必死で理由を探してます。本業の後のフルタイムの仕事ですよ、これ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is. Mine are teenagers now, so it's a different kind of challenge. More talking, less climbing.
script.ja: そうね。うちはもうティーンエイジャーだから、また別の種類の課題があるわ。登るより話す方が多いけどね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I'm not ready for the talking challenges. I'm still trying to get enough sleep for the current ones.
script.ja: 話す方の課題はまだ心の準備ができてないです。今の課題のために、とにかく睡眠をとろうとしてる段階なので。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: This rain makes me think about being somewhere warm and dry. I was thinking about going to Beppu in Oita Prefecture next year.
script.ja: この雨だと、どこか暖かくて乾燥した場所にいることを考えてしまうわ。来年、大分県の別府に行こうかなって思ってるの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, Beppu, the onsen? That sounds nice.
script.ja: おお、別府の温泉ですか？いいですねぇ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes. I haven't been in years. Their hells are quite famous, and the food in Oita is really good too.
script.ja: ええ。もう何年も行ってないのよ。地獄めぐりも有名だし、大分の食べ物も本当に美味しいから。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: That's a good plan. Maybe I'll actually get some sleep there.
script.ja: それは良い計画ですね。もしかしたら、そこで本当に眠れるかもしれない。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Well, that's it for us this morning.
script.ja: さて、今朝はここまでね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Have a good day, everyone. Try not to get too drenched out there.
script.ja: 皆さん、良い一日を。外でびしょ濡れにならないように気をつけてくださいね。

news.title: Hiroko Machida brings Japan’s national treasures in focus with high-quality digital reproduction
news.summary: The Machida Hiroko Interior Coordinator Academy, a leading interior design college and architecture practise, is launching a range of interior art featuring Japan’s most treasured artworks. The academy…

news.title: Making the ordinary extraordinary with Keisuke Unosawa
news.summary: The story of Keisuke Unosawa’s design career is far from ordinary. You won’t find skills developed at a prestigious design school or an expensive intern program abroad; there…

news.title: Clothing company to bring popular Japanese mobile games’ character costumes to life
news.summary: We can’t get enough of cosplay, so we welcome news that clothes company Nutte, in conjunction with mobile phone game makers Axel Mark, have obtained an official license…

wikipedia.title: 劇画の日
wikipedia.description: 1964（昭和39）年、劇画雑誌「ガロ」が創刊された。白土三平の「カムイ伝」など、多くの話題作がこの雑誌から生まれた。
